render.c(3) Library Functions Manual render.c(3)

NAME

render.c - 🎨 Per-client rendering threads: 60fps video and 100fps audio processing with rate limiting

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES 960

Functions


void * client_video_render_thread (void *arg)
Interruptible sleep function with platform-specific optimizations. void * client_audio_render_thread (void *arg)
Main audio rendering thread function for individual clients. int create_client_render_threads (server_context_t *server_ctx, client_info_t *client)
Create and initialize per-client rendering threads. void stop_client_render_threads (client_info_t *client)
Stop and cleanup per-client rendering threads.

Detailed Description

🎨 Per-client rendering threads: 60fps video and 100fps audio processing with rate limiting

1. Manage per-client video rendering threads (60fps per client))Manage per-client audio rendering threads (100fps per client, 480 samples @ 48kHz))Coordinate frame generation timing and rate limitinglimitingEnsure thread-safe access to client state and media buffersbuffersProvide graceful thread lifecycle managementmanagementHandle platform-specific timing and synchronization THREADING ARCHITECTURE:

PER-CLIENT THREAD MODEL: Each connected client spawns exactly 2 dedicated threads:

1.
VIDEO RENDER THREAD:
•
Generates personalized ASCII frames at 60fps
•
Calls stream.c functions for video mixing and conversion
•
Queues frames in client's video packet queue
•
Adapts to client's terminal capabilities
2.
AUDIO RENDER THREAD:
•
Mixes audio streams excluding client's own audio
•
Processes audio at 100fps (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
•
Queues audio packets in client's audio packet queue
•
Provides low-latency audio delivery

PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:

LINEAR SCALING:

  • Each client gets dedicated CPU resources
  • No shared bottlenecks between clients
  • Performance scales linearly up to 9+ clients
  • Real-time guarantees maintained per client

TIMING PRECISION:

  • Video: 16.67ms intervals (60fps)
  • Audio: 10ms intervals (100fps, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
  • Platform-specific high-resolution timers
  • Interruptible sleep for responsive shutdown

RATE LIMITING STRATEGY:

VIDEO RATE LIMITING:

  • Uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC for precise timing
  • Calculates elapsed time since last frame
  • Sleeps only if ahead of schedule
  • Prevents CPU spinning under light load

AUDIO RATE LIMITING:

  • Fixed 10ms intervals to match 48kHz sample rate (480 samples/iteration)
  • Accumulates to 960 samples (20ms) for Opus encoding
  • Ensures smooth audio delivery
  • Balances latency vs CPU usage

THREAD SAFETY AND SYNCHRONIZATION:

PER-CLIENT MUTEX PROTECTION: All client state access uses the snapshot pattern:

1.
Acquire client->client_state_mutex
2.
Copy needed state to local variables
3.
Release mutex immediately
4.
Process using local copies

Synchronization Points:

  • Thread running flags (protected by client mutex)
  • Client dimensions and capabilities (atomic snapshots)
  • Packet queue access (internally thread-safe)
  • Buffer access (uses buffer-specific locks)

GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN HANDLING:

SHUTDOWN SEQUENCE:

1.
Set thread running flags to false
2.
Threads detect flag change and exit loops
3.
Join threads to ensure complete cleanup
4.
Destroy per-client mutexes
5.
Clear thread handles

INTERRUPTIBLE OPERATIONS:

  • Sleep operations can be interrupted by shutdown signal
  • Threads check shutdown flag frequently
  • No blocking operations that can't be interrupted

PLATFORM ABSTRACTION INTEGRATION:

CROSS-PLATFORM TIMING:

  • Windows: Uses Sleep() with millisecond precision
  • POSIX: Uses condition variables for responsive interruption
  • High-resolution timing with clock_gettime()

THREAD MANAGEMENT:

  • Uses platform abstraction layer for thread creation/joining
  • Handles platform-specific thread initialization
  • Safe thread cleanup across all platforms

ERROR HANDLING PHILOSOPHY:

  • Thread creation failures trigger complete cleanup
  • Invalid client states cause thread exit (not crash)
  • Memory allocation failures are logged and handled gracefully
  • Network errors don't affect thread stability

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • stream.c: Called by video threads for frame generation
  • mixer.c: Called by audio threads for audio mixing
  • client.c: Manages thread creation/destruction lifecycle
  • packet_queue.c: Used for queuing generated media
  • main.c: Provides global shutdown signaling

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

The original server.c contained rendering logic mixed with connection management, making it impossible to:

  • Understand performance characteristics
  • Optimize rendering pipelines
  • Add new rendering features
  • Debug threading issues

This separation provides:

  • Clear rendering pipeline architecture
  • Isolated performance optimization
  • Easier threading model comprehension
  • Better real-time guarantees

MEMORY AND RESOURCE MANAGEMENT:

  • All allocations are bounded and predictable
  • Thread stacks are platform-managed
  • Frame generation uses buffer pools
  • No memory leaks on thread exit
  • Automatic resource cleanup on client disconnect

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

See also

stream.c For Video frame generation implementation

mixer.c For Audio mixing implementation

client.c For thread lifecycle management

Definition in file server/render.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES 960

Function Documentation

void * client_audio_render_thread (void * arg)

Main audio rendering thread function for individual clients. This is the audio processing thread that generates personalized audio mixes for a specific client at 100fps (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz). Each client receives audio from all other clients while excluding their own audio to prevent echo and feedback.

THREAD EXECUTION FLOW:

1.
INITIALIZATION:
•
Validate client parameters and socket
•
Allocate local mixing buffer
•
Log thread startup for debugging
2.
MAIN PROCESSING LOOP:
•
Check thread running state (with mutex protection)
•
Take atomic snapshot of client state
•
Generate audio mix excluding this client's audio
•
Queue mixed audio for delivery to client
•
Sleep for precise timing (10ms intervals)
3.
CLEANUP AND EXIT:
•
Log thread termination
•
Return NULL to indicate clean exit

AUDIO MIXING STRATEGY:

ECHO PREVENTION:

  • Excludes client's own audio from their mix
  • Prevents audio feedback loops
  • Uses mixer_process_excluding_source() function
  • Maintains audio quality for other participants

PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:

TIMING PRECISION:

  • Target: 100fps (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
  • Accumulates to 960 samples (20ms) for Opus encoding
  • Fixed timing (no dynamic rate adjustment)
  • Low-latency audio delivery

BUFFER MANAGEMENT:

  • Uses fixed-size local buffer (AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER)
  • No dynamic allocation in processing loop
  • Predictable memory usage
  • Cache-friendly processing pattern

THREAD SAFETY MECHANISMS:

STATE SYNCHRONIZATION:

  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for state access
  • Implements snapshot pattern for client ID and queues
  • Prevents race conditions during client disconnect
  • Safe concurrent access with other threads

MIXER COORDINATION:

  • Global audio mixer is internally thread-safe
  • Multiple audio threads can process concurrently
  • Lock-free audio buffer operations where possible

AUDIO PIPELINE INTEGRATION:

AUDIO MIXING:

  • Uses global g_audio_mixer for multi-client processing
  • Processes AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER samples per iteration
  • Handles varying number of active audio sources

AUDIO DELIVERY:

  • Queues audio directly in client's audio packet queue
  • Higher priority than video packets in send thread
  • Real-time delivery requirements

ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY:

  • Invalid client parameters: Log error and exit immediately
  • Missing audio mixer: Continue with polling (mixer may initialize later)
  • Queue failures: Log debug info and continue (expected under load)
  • Client disconnect: Clean exit without error spam

PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATIONS:

  • Local buffer allocation (no malloc in loop)
  • Minimal processing per iteration
  • Efficient mixer integration
  • Low-overhead packet queuing

Parameters

arg Pointer to client_info_t for the target client

Returns

NULL on thread completion (always clean exit)

Note

This function runs in its own dedicated thread

Audio processing has higher priority than video

Thread lifetime matches client connection lifetime

Warning

Invalid client parameter causes immediate thread termination

Thread must be joined properly to prevent resource leaks

See also

mixer_process_excluding_source() For Audio mixing implementation

AUDIO_FRAMES_PER_BUFFER For buffer size constant

Definition at line 763 of file server/render.c.

763                                             {
764   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
765 
766   if (!client) {
767     log_error("Invalid client info in audio render thread");
768     return NULL;
769   }
770 
771   // Take snapshot of client ID and display name at start to avoid race conditions
772   char thread_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
773   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_client_id, client->client_id, sizeof(thread_client_id) - 1);
774   char thread_display_name[64];
775   bool is_webrtc = (client->socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
776   (void)is_webrtc; // May be unused in release builds
777 
778   // LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Only need client_state_mutex, not global rwlock
779   // We already have a stable client pointer
780   mutex_lock(&client->client_state_mutex);
781   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_display_name, client->display_name, sizeof(thread_display_name));
782   mutex_unlock(&client->client_state_mutex);
783 
784 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
785   log_debug("Audio render thread started for client %u (%s), webrtc=%d", thread_client_id, thread_display_name,
786             is_webrtc);
787 #endif
788 
789   // Mix buffer: up to 960 samples for adaptive reading
790   // Normal: 480 samples = 10ms @ 48kHz
791   // Catchup: 960 samples = 20ms when buffers are filling up
792   float mix_buffer[960];
793 
794 // Opus frame accumulation buffer (960 samples = 20ms @ 48kHz)
795 // Opus requires minimum 480 samples, 960 is optimal for 20ms frames
796 #define OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES 960
797   float opus_frame_buffer[OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES];
798   int opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
799 
800   // Create Opus encoder for this client's audio stream (48kHz, mono, 128kbps, AUDIO mode for music quality)
801   opus_codec_t *opus_encoder = opus_codec_create_encoder(OPUS_APPLICATION_AUDIO, 48000, 128000);
802   if (!opus_encoder) {
803     log_error("Failed to create Opus encoder for audio render thread (client %u)", thread_client_id);
804     return NULL;
805   }
806 
807   // FPS tracking for audio render thread
808   fps_t audio_fps_tracker = {0};
809   fps_init(&audio_fps_tracker, AUDIO_RENDER_FPS, "SERVER AUDIO");
810 
811   // Adaptive sleep for audio rate limiting at 100 FPS (10ms intervals, 480 samples @ 48kHz)
812   adaptive_sleep_state_t audio_sleep_state = {0};
813   adaptive_sleep_config_t audio_config = {
814       .baseline_sleep_ns = 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, // 10ms = 100 FPS (480 samples @ 48kHz)
815       .min_speed_multiplier = 1.0,             // Constant rate (no slowdown)
816       .max_speed_multiplier = 1.0,             // Constant rate (no speedup)
817       .speedup_rate = 0.0,                     // No adaptive behavior (constant rate)
818       .slowdown_rate = 0.0                     // No adaptive behavior (constant rate)
819   };
820   adaptive_sleep_init(&audio_sleep_state, &audio_config);
821 
822   // Per-thread counters (NOT static - each thread instance gets its own)
823   int backpressure_check_counter = 0;
824   int server_audio_frame_count = 0;
825 
826   bool should_continue = true;
827   while (should_continue && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
828     log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Audio render loop iteration for client %u", thread_client_id);
829 
830     // Check for immediate shutdown
831     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
832       log_debug("Audio render thread stopping for client %u (g_should_exit)", thread_client_id);
833       break;
834     }
835 
836     // Check thread state before acquiring any locks to prevent use-after-destroy.
837     // If we acquire locks after client is being destroyed, we'll crash with SIGSEGV
838     should_continue = (((int)atomic_load_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running) != 0) &&
839                        ((int)atomic_load_bool(&client->active) != 0) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down));
840 
841     if (!should_continue) {
842       log_debug("Audio render thread stopping for client %u (should_continue=false)", thread_client_id);
843       break;
844     }
845 
846     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
847       log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Audio render waiting for mixer (client %u)", thread_client_id);
848       // Check shutdown flag while waiting
849       if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit))
850         break;
851       platform_sleep_ns(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
852       continue;
853     }
854 
855     // Optimization: No mutex needed - all fields are atomic or stable.
856     // client_id: atomic_t - use atomic_load for thread safety
857     // active: atomic_t - use atomic_load
858     // audio_queue: Assigned once at init and never changes
859     const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;         // Atomic read
860     bool active_snapshot = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);        // Atomic read
861     packet_queue_t *audio_queue_snapshot = client->audio_queue; // Stable after init
862 
863     // Check if client is still active after getting snapshot
864     if (!active_snapshot || !audio_queue_snapshot) {
865       break;
866     }
867 
868     // Create mix excluding THIS client's audio using snapshot data
869     START_TIMER("mix_%s", client_id_snapshot);
870 
871     // ADAPTIVE READING: Read more samples when we're behind to catch up
872     // Normal: 480 samples per 10ms iteration
873     // When behind: read up to 960 samples to catch up faster
874     // Check source buffer levels to decide
875     int samples_to_read = 480; // Default: 10ms worth
876 
877     // Log latency at each stage in the server pipeline
878     // NOTE: Skipping mixer source latency logging due to potential use-after-free
879     // when source IDs are freed during concurrent client cleanup
880     if (g_audio_mixer) {
881       // Log outgoing queue latency
882       size_t queue_depth = packet_queue_size(audio_queue_snapshot);
883       float queue_latency_ms = (float)queue_depth * 20.0f; // ~20ms per Opus packet
884       log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "LATENCY: Server send queue for client %s: %.1fms (%zu packets)",
885                     client_id_snapshot, queue_latency_ms, queue_depth);
886     }
887 
888     int samples_mixed = 0;
889     if (GET_OPTION(no_audio_mixer)) {
890       // Disable mixer.h processing: simple mixing without ducking/compression/etc
891       // Just add audio from all sources except this client, no processing
892       SAFE_MEMSET(mix_buffer, samples_to_read * sizeof(float), 0, samples_to_read * sizeof(float));
893 
894       if (g_audio_mixer) {
895         int max_samples_in_frame = 0;
896         // Simple mixing: just add all sources except current client
897         for (int i = 0; i < g_audio_mixer->max_sources; i++) {
898           if (g_audio_mixer->source_ids[i] && strcmp(g_audio_mixer->source_ids[i], client_id_snapshot) != 0 &&
899               g_audio_mixer->source_buffers[i]) {
900             // Read from this source and add to mix buffer
901             float temp_buffer[960]; // Max adaptive read size
902             int samples_read =
903                 (int)audio_ring_buffer_read(g_audio_mixer->source_buffers[i], temp_buffer, samples_to_read);
904 
905             // Track the maximum samples we got from any source
906             if (samples_read > max_samples_in_frame) {
907               max_samples_in_frame = samples_read;
908             }
909 
910             // Add to mix buffer
911             for (int j = 0; j < samples_read; j++) {
912               mix_buffer[j] += temp_buffer[j];
913             }
914           }
915         }
916         samples_mixed = max_samples_in_frame; // Only count samples we actually read
917       }
918 
919       log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
920                       "Audio mixer DISABLED (--no-audio-mixer): simple mixing, samples=%d for client %u", samples_mixed,
921                       client_id_snapshot);
922     } else {
923       // Use adaptive sample count in normal mixer mode
924       uint32_t client_id_hash = fnv1a_hash_string(client_id_snapshot);
925       samples_mixed = mixer_process_excluding_source(g_audio_mixer, mix_buffer, samples_to_read, client_id_hash);
926     }
927 
928     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "mix_%s", "Mixer for client %s: took",
929                              client_id_snapshot);
930 
931     // Debug logging every 100 iterations (disabled - can slow down audio rendering)
932     // log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "Audio render for client %u: samples_mixed=%d", client_id_snapshot,
933     // samples_mixed);
934 
935     // Accumulate all samples (including 0 or partial) until we have a full Opus frame
936     // This maintains continuous stream without silence padding
937     START_TIMER("accum_%s", client_id_snapshot);
938 
939     int space_available = OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES - opus_frame_accumulated;
940     int samples_to_copy = (samples_mixed <= space_available) ? samples_mixed : space_available;
941 
942     // Only copy if we have samples, otherwise just wait for next frame
943     if (samples_to_copy > 0) {
944       SAFE_MEMCPY(opus_frame_buffer + opus_frame_accumulated,
945                   (OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES - opus_frame_accumulated) * sizeof(float), mix_buffer,
946                   samples_to_copy * sizeof(float));
947       opus_frame_accumulated += samples_to_copy;
948     }
949 
950     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 2 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "accum_%s", "Accumulate for client %s: took",
951                              client_id_snapshot);
952 
953     // Only encode and send when we have accumulated a full Opus frame
954     if (opus_frame_accumulated >= OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES) {
955       // OPTIMIZATION: Don't check queue depth every iteration - it's expensive (requires lock)
956       // Only check periodically every 100 iterations (~0.6s at 172 fps)
957       // NOTE: backpressure_check_counter is now per-thread (not static), so each client thread has its own counter
958       bool apply_backpressure = false;
959 
960       if (++backpressure_check_counter >= 100) {
961         backpressure_check_counter = 0;
962         size_t queue_depth = packet_queue_size(audio_queue_snapshot);
963         // Opus frames are produced at ~50 FPS (20ms each)
964         // Reduced threshold from 50 to 10 packets (~200ms buffer) to prevent audio gaps
965         // On localhost, queue shouldn't back up at all; aggressive thresholds cause frame skipping
966         apply_backpressure = (queue_depth > 10); // > 10 packets = ~200ms buffered at 50 FPS
967 
968         if (apply_backpressure) {
969           log_warn_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
970                          "Audio backpressure for client %s: queue depth %zu packets (%.1fms buffered)",
971                          client_id_snapshot, queue_depth, (float)queue_depth / 50.0f * 1000.0f);
972         }
973       }
974 
975       if (apply_backpressure) {
976         // Skip this packet to let the queue drain
977         // Reset accumulation buffer so fresh samples can be captured on next iteration.
978         // Without this reset, we'd loop forever with stale audio and no space for new samples
979         opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
980         // Reduced sleep from 5.8ms to 1ms to minimize audio gaps on localhost
981         platform_sleep_ns(1 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
982         continue;
983       }
984 
985       // Encode accumulated Opus frame (960 samples = 20ms @ 48kHz)
986       uint8_t opus_buffer[1024]; // Max Opus frame size
987 
988       START_TIMER("opus_encode_%s", client_id_snapshot);
989 
990       int opus_size =
991           opus_codec_encode(opus_encoder, opus_frame_buffer, OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES, opus_buffer, sizeof(opus_buffer));
992 
993       STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "opus_encode_%s",
994                                "Opus encode for client %s: took", client_id_snapshot);
995 
996       // DEBUG: Log mix buffer and encoding results to see audio levels being sent
997       {
998         float peak = 0.0f, rms = 0.0f;
999         for (int i = 0; i < OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES; i++) {
1000           float abs_val = fabsf(opus_frame_buffer[i]);
1001           if (abs_val > peak)
1002             peak = abs_val;
1003           rms += opus_frame_buffer[i] * opus_frame_buffer[i];
1004         }
1005         rms = sqrtf(rms / OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES);
1006         // NOTE: server_audio_frame_count is now per-thread (not static), so each client thread has its own counter
1007         server_audio_frame_count++;
1008         if (server_audio_frame_count <= 5 || server_audio_frame_count % 20 == 0) {
1009           // Log first 4 samples to verify they look like valid audio (not NaN/Inf/garbage)
1010           log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT,
1011                         "Server audio frame #%d for client %u: samples_mixed=%d, Peak=%.6f, RMS=%.6f, opus_size=%d, "
1012                         "first4=[%.4f,%.4f,%.4f,%.4f]",
1013                         server_audio_frame_count, client_id_snapshot, samples_mixed, peak, rms, opus_size,
1014                         opus_frame_buffer[0], opus_frame_buffer[1], opus_frame_buffer[2], opus_frame_buffer[3]);
1015         }
1016       }
1017 
1018       // Always reset accumulation buffer after attempting to encode - we've consumed these samples
1019       // If we don't reset, new audio samples would be dropped while stale data sits in the buffer
1020       opus_frame_accumulated = 0;
1021 
1022       if (opus_size <= 0) {
1023         log_error("Failed to encode audio to Opus for client %s: opus_size=%d", client_id_snapshot, opus_size);
1024       } else {
1025         // Wrap Opus data in proper batch packet format for client parsing:
1026         // Header: sample_rate (4B) + frame_duration (4B) + frame_count (4B)
1027         // Frame sizes: frame_count * 2B (one uint16 per frame with Opus data size)
1028         // Opus data: raw encoded audio
1029 
1030         // Fixed values for Opus encoding (standard 48kHz, 960 samples per frame = 20ms)
1031         const uint32_t sample_rate = 48000;
1032         const uint32_t frame_duration = 20; // milliseconds (960 samples / 48kHz = 20ms)
1033         const uint32_t frame_count = 1;     // one Opus frame per packet
1034         const uint16_t frame_size = (uint16_t)opus_size;
1035 
1036         // Calculate total packet size: 16-byte header + frame_sizes + opus data
1037         const size_t header_size = 16; // 4 uint32s (sample_rate, frame_duration, frame_count, reserved)
1038         const size_t frame_sizes_size = (size_t)frame_count * sizeof(uint16_t);
1039         const size_t total_packet_size = header_size + frame_sizes_size + (size_t)opus_size;
1040 
1041         // Allocate buffer for the complete packet
1042         uint8_t *packet_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(total_packet_size, uint8_t *);
1043         if (!packet_buffer) {
1044           log_error("Failed to allocate Opus batch packet buffer for client %s (size=%zu)", client_id_snapshot,
1045                     total_packet_size);
1046         } else {
1047           // Build header in network byte order
1048           uint8_t *ptr = packet_buffer;
1049 
1050           // Write sample_rate (4 bytes, network byte order)
1051           uint32_t sr_net = htonl(sample_rate);
1052           memcpy(ptr, &sr_net, 4);
1053           ptr += 4;
1054 
1055           // Write frame_duration (4 bytes, network byte order)
1056           uint32_t fd_net = htonl(frame_duration);
1057           memcpy(ptr, &fd_net, 4);
1058           ptr += 4;
1059 
1060           // Write frame_count (4 bytes, network byte order)
1061           uint32_t fc_net = htonl(frame_count);
1062           memcpy(ptr, &fc_net, 4);
1063           ptr += 4;
1064 
1065           // Write reserved field (4 bytes, all zeros)
1066           uint32_t reserved = 0;
1067           memcpy(ptr, &reserved, 4);
1068           ptr += 4;
1069 
1070           // Write frame sizes (2 bytes per frame, network byte order)
1071           for (uint32_t i = 0; i < frame_count; i++) {
1072             uint16_t fs_net = htons(frame_size);
1073             memcpy(ptr, &fs_net, 2);
1074             ptr += 2;
1075           }
1076 
1077           // Copy Opus data
1078           memcpy(ptr, opus_buffer, (size_t)opus_size);
1079 
1080           // Queue the properly formatted packet
1081           START_TIMER("audio_queue_%s", client_id_snapshot);
1082 
1083           int result = packet_queue_enqueue(audio_queue_snapshot, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_buffer,
1084                                             total_packet_size, 0, true);
1085 
1086           STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, NS_PER_SEC_INT, 1 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "audio_queue_%s",
1087                                    "Audio queue for client %s: took", client_id_snapshot);
1088 
1089           if (result < 0) {
1090             log_debug("Failed to queue Opus audio for client %s", client_id_snapshot);
1091           } else {
1092             // FPS tracking - audio packet successfully queued (handles lag detection and periodic reporting)
1093             fps_frame_ns(&audio_fps_tracker, time_get_ns(), "audio packet queued");
1094           }
1095 
1096           // Free the allocated packet buffer after queuing (packet_queue_enqueue copies data)
1097           SAFE_FREE(packet_buffer);
1098         }
1099       }
1100       // NOTE: opus_frame_accumulated is already reset at line 928 after encode attempt
1101     }
1102 
1103     // Audio mixing rate limiting using adaptive sleep system
1104     // Target: 10ms intervals (100 FPS) for 480 samples @ 48kHz
1105     // Use queue_depth=0 and target_depth=0 for constant-rate audio processing
1106     adaptive_sleep_do(&audio_sleep_state, 0, 0);
1107   }
1108 
1109 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1110   log_debug("Audio render thread stopped for client %u", thread_client_id);
1111 #endif
1112 
1113   // Clean up Opus encoder
1114   if (opus_encoder) {
1115     opus_codec_destroy(opus_encoder);
1116   }
1117 
1118   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
1119   asciichat_errno_destroy();
1120 
1121   return NULL;
1122 }




References client_info::active, adaptive_sleep_do(),
    adaptive_sleep_init(), asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    atomic_load_bool(), client_info::audio_queue,
    AUDIO_RENDER_FPS, client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    audio_ring_buffer_read(), client_info::client_id,
    client_info::client_state_mutex, client_info::display_name,
    fps_frame_ns(), fps_init(), g_audio_mixer,
    g_should_exit, GET_OPTION, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    log_error, LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, LOG_RATE_SLOW,
    log_warn_every, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN,
    mixer_process_excluding_source(), mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT,
    OPUS_APPLICATION_AUDIO, opus_codec_create_encoder(),
    opus_codec_destroy(), opus_codec_encode(),
    OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES, packet_queue_enqueue(),
    packet_queue_size(), PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH,
    platform_sleep_ns(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_MEMCPY, SAFE_MEMSET, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    client_info::shutting_down, client_info::socket,
    START_TIMER, STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by create_client_render_threads().




void
  * client_video_render_thread (void * arg)


Interruptible sleep function with platform-specific optimizations.
    Provides a sleep mechanism that can be interrupted by the global shutdown
    signal, enabling responsive thread termination. The implementation varies by
    platform to optimize for responsiveness vs CPU usage.










PLATFORM-SPECIFIC
  BEHAVIOR: 


WINDOWS IMPLEMENTATION:


    
  
  • Uses Sleep() API with millisecond precision
    • 
  
  • Simple approach due to Windows timer characteristics
    • 
  
  • Sleep(1) may sleep up to 15.6ms due to timer resolution
    • 
  
  • Checks shutdown flag before and after sleep
    • 



POSIX IMPLEMENTATION (Linux/macOS):


    
  
  • Uses condition variables for precise timing
    • 
  
  • Can be interrupted immediately by shutdown signal
    • 
  
  • Higher responsiveness for thread termination
    • 
  
  • Uses static mutex/condition variable for coordination
    • 







USAGE
  PATTERNS: 


    
  
  • Rate limiting in render threads (maintain target FPS)
    • 
  
  • Backoff delays when queues are full
    • 
  
  • Polling intervals for state changes
    • 
  
  • Graceful busy-wait alternatives
    • 



PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS:


    
  
  • Low CPU overhead (proper sleep, not busy wait)
    • 
  
  • Sub-second responsiveness to shutdown
    • 
  
  • Maintains timing precision for media processing
    • 
  
  • Scales well with many concurrent threads
    • 



ERROR HANDLING:


    
  
  • Early return if shutdown flag is set
    • 
  
  • Platform-specific error handling for sleep APIs
    • 
  
  • Logging for debugging (throttled to avoid spam)
    • 



Parameters


usec Sleep duration in microseconds


Note


Function may return early if shutdown is requested



Minimum sleep time is platform-dependent (typically 1ms)




Static variables are used for call counting (debugging)




Warning


Not suitable for precise timing (use for rate limiting
  only)


See also


VIDEO_RENDER_FPS For Video thread timing
  requirements


Main video rendering thread function for individual clients




This is the core video processing thread that generates
    personalized ASCII art frames for a specific client at 60fps. Each connected
    client gets their own dedicated video thread, providing linear performance
    scaling and personalized rendering based on terminal capabilities.








THREAD EXECUTION FLOW: 



  
1.

  
INITIALIZATION:

  
 •

  
Validate client parameters and socket

  
 •

  
Initialize timing variables for rate limiting

  
 •

  
Log thread startup for debugging

  
2.

  
MAIN PROCESSING LOOP:

  
 •

  
Check thread running state (with mutex protection)

  
 •

  
Rate limit to 60fps using high-resolution timing

  
 •

  
Take atomic snapshot of client state

  
 •

  
Generate personalized ASCII frame for this client

  
 •

  
Queue frame for delivery to client

  
 •

  
Update timing for next iteration

  
3.

  
CLEANUP AND EXIT:

  
 •

  
Log thread termination

  
 •

  
Return NULL to indicate clean exit








PERFORMANCE CHARACTERISTICS: 


TIMING PRECISION:


    
  
  • Target: 60fps (16.67ms intervals)
    • 
  
  • Uses CLOCK_MONOTONIC for accurate timing
    • 
  
  • Only sleeps if ahead of schedule (no CPU waste)
    • 
  
  • Maintains consistent frame rate under varying load
    • 



CLIENT-SPECIFIC PROCESSING:


    
  
  • Generates frames customized for client's terminal
    • 
  
  • Respects color depth, palette, and size preferences
    • 
  
  • Adapts to client capability changes dynamically
    • 
  
  • Handles client disconnection gracefully
    • 







THREAD SAFETY MECHANISMS: 


STATE SYNCHRONIZATION:


    
  
  • Uses client->client_state_mutex for all state access
    • 
  
  • Implements snapshot pattern (copy state, release lock)
    • 
  
  • Prevents race conditions with other threads
    • 
  
  • Safe concurrent access to client data
    • 



SHUTDOWN COORDINATION:


    
  
  • Monitors global g_should_exit flag
    • 
  
  • Checks per-client running flags with mutex protection
    • 
  
  • Responds to shutdown within one frame interval (16.67ms)
    • 







VIDEO
  PIPELINE INTEGRATION: 


FRAME GENERATION:


    
  
  • Calls create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() from
    stream.c
    • 
  
  • Provides client-specific dimensions and preferences
    • 
  
  • Handles frame generation failures gracefully
    • 



FRAME DELIVERY:


    
  
  • Calls queue_ascii_frame_for_client() for packet queuing
    • 
  
  • Uses client's dedicated video packet queue
    • 
  
  • Send thread delivers frames asynchronously
    • 







ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY: 


    
  
  • Invalid client parameters: Log error and exit immediately
    • 
  
  • Frame generation failures: Continue with debug logging
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow: Continue with debug logging (expected under load)
    • 
  
  • Client disconnect: Clean exit without error spam
    • 
  
  • Timing issues: Self-correcting (no accumulation)
    • 







LOGGING
  AND MONITORING: 


    
  
  • Success logging: Throttled (every ~4 seconds)
    • 
  
  • Failure logging: Throttled (every ~10 seconds)
    • 
  
  • Performance metrics: Frame count and size tracking
    • 
  
  • Debug information: Timing and queue status
    • 



Parameters


arg Pointer to client_info_t for the target
  client


Returns


NULL on thread completion (always clean exit)


Note


This function runs in its own dedicated thread



Thread lifetime matches client connection lifetime




Memory allocated for frames is automatically freed




Warning


Invalid client parameter causes immediate thread
  termination



Thread must be joined properly to prevent resource leaks




See also


create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client() For
  frame generation



queue_ascii_frame_for_client() For frame delivery




VIDEO_RENDER_FPS For timing constant definition




Definition at line 339 of file server/render.c.


339                                             {
340   client_info_t *client = (client_info_t *)arg;
341   if (!client) {
342     log_error("NULL client pointer in video render thread");
343     return NULL;
344   }
345 
346   // Take snapshot of client ID and socket at start to avoid race conditions
347   char thread_client_id[MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
348   SAFE_STRNCPY(thread_client_id, client->client_id, sizeof(thread_client_id) - 1);
349   socket_t thread_socket = client->socket;
350   bool is_webrtc = (thread_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE);
351   (void)is_webrtc; // May be unused in release builds
352 
353   log_debug("Video render thread: client_id=%s, webrtc=%d", thread_client_id, is_webrtc);
354 
355   log_info("[VIDEO_RENDER_THREAD_START] ★★★ LOCK STATE at thread entry for client %u", thread_client_id);
356   // debug_sync_print_state();  // Disabled: causes AddressSanitizer stack-use-after-return crash
357 
358   // Wait for client to send terminal capabilities before rendering
359   // Without capabilities, convert_composite_to_ascii() will fail
360   int timeout_ms = 5000; // 5 second timeout
361   int waited_ms = 0;
362   while (!client->has_terminal_caps && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
363     if (waited_ms == 0) {
364       log_debug("Waiting for terminal capabilities from client %u...", thread_client_id);
365     }
366     platform_sleep_ms(10);
367     waited_ms += 10;
368     if (waited_ms >= timeout_ms) {
369       log_warn("Timeout waiting for terminal capabilities from client %u (waited %dms)", thread_client_id, waited_ms);
370       return NULL; // Exit thread if capabilities never arrive
371     }
372   }
373   if (client->has_terminal_caps) {
374     log_debug("Received terminal capabilities for client %u after %dms", thread_client_id, waited_ms);
375   }
376 
377   // Get client's desired FPS from capabilities or use default
378   int client_fps = VIDEO_RENDER_FPS; // Default to 60 FPS
379   // Use snapshot pattern to avoid mutex in render thread
380   bool has_caps = client->has_terminal_caps;
381   int desired_fps = has_caps ? client->terminal_caps.desired_fps : 0;
382   if (has_caps && desired_fps > 0) {
383     client_fps = desired_fps;
384     log_debug("Client %u requested FPS: %d (has_caps=%d, desired_fps=%d)", thread_client_id, client_fps, has_caps,
385               desired_fps);
386   } else {
387     log_debug("Client %u using default FPS: %d (has_caps=%d, desired_fps=%d)", thread_client_id, client_fps, has_caps,
388               desired_fps);
389   }
390 
391   int base_frame_interval_ms = 1000 / client_fps;
392   log_debug("Client %u render interval: %dms (%d FPS)", thread_client_id, base_frame_interval_ms, client_fps);
393 
394   // FPS tracking for video render thread
395   fps_t video_fps_tracker = {0};
396   fps_init(&video_fps_tracker, client_fps, "SERVER VIDEO");
397 
398   // Adaptive sleep for frame rate limiting
399   adaptive_sleep_state_t sleep_state = {0};
400   adaptive_sleep_config_t config = {
401       .baseline_sleep_ns = (uint64_t)(NS_PER_SEC_INT / client_fps), // Dynamic FPS (typically 16.67ms for 60 FPS)
402       .min_speed_multiplier = 1.0,                                  // Constant rate (no slowdown)
403       .max_speed_multiplier = 1.0,                                  // Constant rate (no speedup)
404       .speedup_rate = 0.0,                                          // No adaptive behavior (constant FPS)
405       .slowdown_rate = 0.0                                          // No adaptive behavior (constant FPS)
406   };
407   adaptive_sleep_init(&sleep_state, &config);
408 
409   log_info("Video render loop STARTING for client %u", thread_client_id);
410 
411   bool should_continue = true;
412   while (should_continue && !atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down)) {
413     START_TIMER("render_iteration");
414     log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Video render loop iteration for client %u", thread_client_id);
415 
416     // Check for immediate shutdown
417     if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
418       log_debug("Video render thread stopping for client %u (g_should_exit)", thread_client_id);
419       break;
420     }
421 
422     bool video_running = atomic_load_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running);
423     bool active = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);
424     bool shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down);
425 
426     should_continue = video_running && active && !shutting_down;
427 
428     if (!should_continue) {
429       log_debug("Video render thread stopping for client %u (should_continue=false: video_running=%d, active=%d, "
430                 "shutting_down=%d)",
431                 thread_client_id, video_running, active, shutting_down);
432       break;
433     }
434 
435     // Frame rate limiting using adaptive sleep system
436     // Use queue_depth=0 and target_depth=0 for constant-rate renderer (no backlog management)
437     uint64_t iter_start_ns = time_get_ns();
438     START_TIMER("render_adaptive_sleep");
439     adaptive_sleep_do(&sleep_state, 0, 0);
440     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_adaptive_sleep", "adaptive_sleep completed");
441     uint64_t after_sleep_ns = time_get_ns();
442 
443     // Capture timestamp for FPS tracking and frame timestamps
444     uint64_t current_time_ns = time_get_ns();
445 
446     // Check thread state again before acquiring locks (client might have been destroyed during sleep).
447     should_continue = atomic_load_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running) && atomic_load_bool(&client->active) &&
448                       !atomic_load_bool(&client->shutting_down);
449     if (!should_continue) {
450       break;
451     }
452 
453     // Optimization: No mutex needed - all fields are atomic or stable.
454     // client_id: stable string pointer
455     // width/height: stable uint16_t values (set once, never modified)
456     // active: atomic_t - use atomic_load for thread safety
457     const char *client_id_snapshot = client->client_id;            // Stable read
458     unsigned short width_snapshot = client->width;                 // Stable uint16_t
459     unsigned short height_snapshot = client->height;               // Stable uint16_t
460     bool active_snapshot = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);      // Atomic read
461 
462     // Check if client is still active after getting snapshot
463     if (!active_snapshot) {
464       break;
465     }
466 
467     // Phase 2 IMPLEMENTED: Generate frame specifically for THIS client using snapshot data
468     size_t frame_size = 0;
469 
470     // Check if any clients are sending video
471     bool has_video_sources = any_clients_sending_video();
472 
473     // DIAGNOSTIC: Track when video sources become available
474     static bool last_has_sources = false;
475     if (has_video_sources != last_has_sources) {
476       log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u video sources: %s", thread_client_id,
477                has_video_sources ? "AVAILABLE" : "UNAVAILABLE");
478       last_has_sources = has_video_sources;
479     }
480 
481     log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT,
482                     "Video render iteration for client %u: has_video_sources=%d, width=%u, height=%u", thread_client_id,
483                     has_video_sources, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
484 
485     // Use default dimensions if client dimensions not received
486     if (width_snapshot == 0 || height_snapshot == 0) {
487       log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Using default dimensions for client %u (client reported: width=%u, height=%u)",
488                     thread_client_id, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
489       // Use 80x25 as standard terminal size fallback for snapshot/test clients
490       width_snapshot = 80;
491       height_snapshot = 25;
492     }
493 
494     // Generate frames for all clients with valid dimensions, regardless of whether other clients are sending video.
495     // This ensures WebSocket snapshot clients (receive-only mode) receive frames even when no other clients are
496     // sending video.
497     int sources_count = 0; // Track number of video sources in this frame
498 
499     // DIAGNOSTIC: Track every frame generation attempt
500     // Use per-client static variables to track frequency independently
501     static uint32_t frame_gen_count = 0;
502     static uint64_t frame_gen_start_time = 0;
503 
504     frame_gen_count++;
505     if (frame_gen_count == 1) {
506       frame_gen_start_time = current_time_ns;
507     }
508 
509     // Log every 120 attempts (should be ~2 seconds at 60 Hz)
510     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
511       uint64_t elapsed_ns = current_time_ns - frame_gen_start_time;
512       double gen_fps = (120.0 / (elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT));
513       log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u LOOP running at %.1f FPS (120 iterations in %.2fs)", thread_client_id, gen_fps,
514                elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT);
515     }
516 
517     log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "About to call create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client for client %u with dims %ux%u",
518                   thread_client_id, width_snapshot, height_snapshot);
519 
520     // debug_sync_print_state() is too expensive to call every frame (kills FPS)
521 
522     uint64_t frame_create_start_ns = time_get_ns();
523     START_TIMER("render_create_frame");
524     char *ascii_frame = create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client(client_id_snapshot, width_snapshot, height_snapshot, false,
525                                                             &frame_size, NULL, &sources_count);
526     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_create_frame", "create_mixed_ascii_frame completed");
527     uint64_t frame_create_end_ns = time_get_ns();
528 
529     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
530       char sleep_str[32], create_str[32];
531       time_pretty((uint64_t)(after_sleep_ns - iter_start_ns), -1, sleep_str, sizeof(sleep_str));
532       time_pretty((uint64_t)(frame_create_end_ns - frame_create_start_ns), -1, create_str, sizeof(create_str));
533       log_warn("  TIMING: adaptive_sleep=%s, frame_create=%s", sleep_str, create_str);
534     }
535 
536     // Always send frames at configured FPS, even if no external video sources yet
537     // This ensures single-client mode (like testing) still gets continuous frames
538     // The frame data (animated background or test pattern) is still valid content
539 
540     // DEBUG: Log frame generation details
541     static uint32_t last_frame_hash = -1; // Initialize to -1 so first frame is always new
542     uint32_t current_frame_hash = 0;
543     if (ascii_frame && frame_size > 0) {
544       for (size_t i = 0; i < frame_size && i < 1000; i++) {
545         current_frame_hash = (uint32_t)((uint64_t)current_frame_hash * 31 + ((unsigned char *)ascii_frame)[i]);
546       }
547       if (current_frame_hash != last_frame_hash) {
548         log_info("RENDER_FRAME CHANGE: Client %u frame #%zu sources=%d hash=0x%08x (prev=0x%08x)", thread_client_id,
549                  frame_size, sources_count, current_frame_hash, last_frame_hash);
550         last_frame_hash = current_frame_hash;
551       } else {
552         log_dev_every(25000, "RENDER_FRAME DUPLICATE: Client %u frame #%zu sources=%d hash=0x%08x (no change)",
553                       thread_client_id, frame_size, sources_count, current_frame_hash);
554       }
555     }
556 
557     log_dev_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT,
558                   "create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client returned: ascii_frame=%p, frame_size=%zu, sources_count=%d",
559                   (void *)ascii_frame, frame_size, sources_count);
560 
561     // Phase 2 IMPLEMENTED: Write frame to double buffer (never drops!)
562     if (ascii_frame && frame_size > 0) {
563       log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Buffering frame for client %u (size=%zu)", thread_client_id, frame_size);
564       // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE DETECTION: Store source count with frame
565       // Send thread will compare this with last sent count to detect grid changes
566       atomic_store_bool(&client->last_rendered_grid_sources, sources_count);
567 
568       // Use double-buffer system which has its own internal swap_mutex
569       // No external locking needed - the double-buffer is thread-safe by design
570       video_frame_buffer_t *vfb_snapshot = client->outgoing_video_buffer;
571 
572       if (vfb_snapshot) {
573         video_frame_t *write_frame = video_frame_begin_write(vfb_snapshot);
574         if (write_frame) {
575           // Copy ASCII frame data to the back buffer (NOT holding rwlock - just double-buffer's internal lock)
576           if (write_frame->data && frame_size <= vfb_snapshot->allocated_buffer_size) {
577             memcpy(write_frame->data, ascii_frame, frame_size);
578             write_frame->size = frame_size;
579             write_frame->capture_timestamp_ns = current_time_ns;
580 
581             // Always commit frame at configured FPS rate (adaptive_sleep_do enforces timing).
582             // Never skip frames based on content changes.
583             {
584               uint64_t commit_start_ns = time_get_ns();
585               // Commit the frame (swaps buffers atomically using vfb->swap_mutex, NOT rwlock)
586               START_TIMER("render_video_frame_commit");
587               video_frame_commit(vfb_snapshot);
588               STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_video_frame_commit", "video_frame_commit completed");
589               uint64_t commit_end_ns = time_get_ns();
590               char commit_duration_str[32];
591               time_pretty((uint64_t)(commit_end_ns - commit_start_ns), -1, commit_duration_str,
592                           sizeof(commit_duration_str));
593 
594               static uint32_t commits_count = 0;
595               static uint64_t commits_start_time = 0;
596               commits_count++;
597               if (commits_count == 1) {
598                 commits_start_time = commit_end_ns;
599               }
600               if (commits_count % 10 == 0) {
601                 uint64_t elapsed_ns = commit_end_ns - commits_start_time;
602                 double commit_fps = (10.0 / (elapsed_ns / (double)NS_PER_SEC_INT));
603                 log_warn("DIAGNOSTIC: Client %u UNIQUE frames being sent at %.1f FPS (10 commits counted)",
604                          thread_client_id, commit_fps);
605               }
606 
607               log_info("[FRAME_COMMIT_TIMING] Client %u frame commit took %s (hash=0x%08x)", thread_client_id,
608                        commit_duration_str, current_frame_hash);
609 
610               // ARCHITECTURE: Frame transmission is handled EXCLUSIVELY by the send thread
611               // The send thread reads from this buffer and applies proper 60 FPS rate limiting
612               // Do NOT send from render thread - it bypasses rate limiting and causes network congestion
613               // Render thread responsibility: Generate frame → Write to buffer → Done
614               // Send thread responsibility: Read from buffer → Apply 60 FPS pacing → Transmit
615             }
616 
617           } else {
618             log_warn("Frame too large for buffer: %zu > %zu", frame_size, vfb_snapshot->allocated_buffer_size);
619           }
620 
621           // FPS tracking - frame successfully generated (handles lag detection and periodic reporting)
622           fps_frame_ns(&video_fps_tracker, current_time_ns, "frame rendered");
623         }
624       }
625 
626       SAFE_FREE(ascii_frame);
627     } else {
628       // No frame generated (probably no video sources) - this is normal, no error logging needed
629       log_dev_every(10 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "Per-client render: No video sources available for client %u",
630                     client_id_snapshot);
631     }
632 
633     uint64_t iter_end_ns = time_get_ns();
634     if (frame_gen_count % 120 == 0) {
635       char total_str[32];
636       time_pretty((uint64_t)(iter_end_ns - iter_start_ns), -1, total_str, sizeof(total_str));
637       log_warn("  TIMING: TOTAL_ITERATION=%s", total_str);
638     }
639     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 0, "render_iteration", "render_iteration completed (total)");
640   }
641 
642 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
643   log_debug("Video render thread stopped for client %u", thread_client_id);
644 #endif
645 
646   // Clean up thread-local error context before exit
647   asciichat_errno_destroy();
648 
649   return NULL;
650 }






References client_info::active, adaptive_sleep_do(),
    adaptive_sleep_init(),
    video_frame_buffer_t::allocated_buffer_size,
    any_clients_sending_video(), asciichat_errno_destroy(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    video_frame_t::capture_timestamp_ns, client_info::client_id,
    create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client(), video_frame_t::data,
    terminal_capabilities_t::desired_fps, fps_frame_ns(),
    fps_init(), g_should_exit,
    client_info::has_terminal_caps, client_info::height,
    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, client_info::last_rendered_grid_sources,
    log_debug, log_debug_every, log_dev_every,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN, NS_PER_MS_INT, NS_PER_SEC_INT,
    client_info::outgoing_video_buffer, platform_sleep_ms(),
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_STRNCPY, client_info::shutting_down,
    video_frame_t::size, client_info::socket, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG, client_info::terminal_caps,
    time_get_ns(), time_pretty(),
    video_frame_begin_write(), video_frame_commit(),
    VIDEO_RENDER_FPS, client_info::video_render_thread_running,
    and client_info::width.


Referenced by create_client_render_threads().




int
  create_client_render_threads (server_context_t * server_ctx,
  client_info_t * client)


Create and initialize per-client rendering threads. Sets up the
    complete per-client threading infrastructure including both video and audio
    rendering threads plus all necessary synchronization primitives. This
    function is called once per client during connection establishment.










INITIALIZATION
  SEQUENCE: 



  
1.

  
MUTEX INITIALIZATION:

  
 •

  
client_state_mutex: Protects client state variables

  
2.

  
THREAD CREATION:

  
 •

  
Create video rendering thread (60fps)

  
 •

  
Create audio rendering thread (172fps)

  
 •

  
Set running flags with proper synchronization

  
3.

  
ERROR HANDLING:

  
 •

  
Complete cleanup if any step fails

  
 •

  
Prevent partially initialized client state

  
 •

  
Ensure no resource leaks on failure








THREAD
  SYNCHRONIZATION SETUP: 


PER-CLIENT MUTEXES: Each client gets dedicated mutexes for
    fine-grained locking:


    
  
  • Prevents contention between clients
    • 
  
  • Allows concurrent processing of multiple clients
    • 
  
  • Enables lock-free operations within client context
    • 



THREAD RUNNING FLAGS:


    
  
  • Protected by client_state_mutex
    • 
  
  • Allow graceful thread termination
    • 
  
  • Checked frequently by render threads
    • 







ERROR
  RECOVERY: 


PARTIAL FAILURE HANDLING: If thread creation fails partway
    through:


    
  
  • Stop and join any already-created threads
    • 
  
  • Destroy any initialized mutexes
    • 
  
  • Return error code to caller
    • 
  
  • Leave client in clean state for retry or cleanup
    • 



RESOURCE LEAK PREVENTION:


    
  
  • All allocations have corresponding cleanup
    • 
  
  • Thread handles are properly managed
    • 
  
  • Mutex destruction is deterministic
    • 







INTEGRATION
  POINTS: 


    
  
  • Called by add_client() in client.c
    • 
  
  • Threads integrate with stream.c and mixer.c
    • 
  
  • Cleanup handled by stop_client_render_threads()
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for thread creation


Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Note


Function performs complete initialization or complete
  cleanup



Mutexes must be destroyed by
  stop_client_render_threads()




Thread handles must be joined before client destruction




Warning


Partial initialization is not supported - complete
  success or failure


See also


stop_client_render_threads() For cleanup
  implementation


Definition at line 1199 of file server/render.c.


1199                                                                                       {
1200   log_info("★★★ create_client_render_threads() CALLED for client_id=%s", client ? client->client_id : "NULL");
1201 
1202   if (!server_ctx || !client) {
1203     log_error("Cannot create render threads: NULL %s", !server_ctx ? "server_ctx" : "client");
1204     return -1;
1205   }
1206 
1207 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1208   log_debug("Creating render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1209 #endif
1210 
1211   // NOTE: Mutexes are already initialized in add_client() before any threads start
1212   // This prevents race conditions where receive thread tries to use uninitialized mutexes
1213 
1214   // Initialize render thread control flags
1215   // IMPORTANT: Set to true BEFORE creating thread to avoid race condition
1216   // where thread starts and immediately exits because flag is false
1217   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, true);
1218   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, true);
1219 
1220   // Create video rendering thread (stop_id=2, stop after receive thread)
1221   char thread_name[64];
1222   safe_snprintf(thread_name, sizeof(thread_name), "video_render_%s", client->client_id);
1223   asciichat_error_t video_result;
1224 
1225   if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1226     video_result = tcp_server_spawn_thread(server_ctx->tcp_server, client->socket, client_video_render_thread, client,
1227                                            2, thread_name);
1228   } else {
1229     log_debug("THREAD_CREATE: WebRTC/WebSocket render video thread for client %s", client->client_id);
1230     video_result =
1231         asciichat_thread_create(&client->video_render_thread, thread_name, client_video_render_thread, client);
1232   }
1233 
1234   if (video_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1235     // Reset flag since thread creation failed
1236     atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1237     // Mutexes will be destroyed by remove_client() which called us
1238     return -1;
1239   }
1240 
1241   // Create audio rendering thread (stop_id=2, same priority as video)
1242   safe_snprintf(thread_name, sizeof(thread_name), "audio_render_%s", client->client_id);
1243   asciichat_error_t audio_result;
1244 
1245   if (client->is_tcp_client) {
1246     audio_result = tcp_server_spawn_thread(server_ctx->tcp_server, client->socket, client_audio_render_thread, client,
1247                                            2, thread_name);
1248   } else {
1249     log_debug("THREAD_CREATE: WebRTC/WebSocket render audio thread for client %s", client->client_id);
1250     audio_result =
1251         asciichat_thread_create(&client->audio_render_thread, thread_name, client_audio_render_thread, client);
1252   }
1253   if (audio_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1254     // Clean up video thread (atomic operation, no mutex needed)
1255     atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1256     // Reset audio flag since thread creation failed
1257     atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1258     // tcp_server_stop_client_threads() will be called by remove_client()
1259     // to clean up the video thread we just created
1260     // Mutexes will be destroyed by remove_client() which called us
1261     return -1;
1262   }
1263 
1264 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1265   log_debug("Created render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1266 #endif
1267 
1268   return 0;
1269 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), client_info::audio_render_thread,
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    client_audio_render_thread(), client_info::client_id,
    client_video_render_thread(), client_info::is_tcp_client,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, safe_snprintf(),
    client_info::socket, server_context_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_spawn_thread(), client_info::video_render_thread,
    and client_info::video_render_thread_running.




void
  stop_client_render_threads (client_info_t * client)


Stop and cleanup per-client rendering threads. Performs graceful
    shutdown of both video and audio rendering threads for a specific client,
    including proper thread joining and resource cleanup. This function ensures
    deterministic cleanup without resource leaks.










SHUTDOWN
  SEQUENCE: 



  
1.

  
SIGNAL SHUTDOWN:

  
 •

  
Set thread running flags to false

  
 •

  
Threads detect flag change and exit processing loops

  
 •

  
Use mutex protection for atomic flag updates

  
2.

  
THREAD JOINING:

  
 •

  
Wait for video render thread to complete

  
 •

  
Wait for audio render thread to complete

  
 •

  
Handle join failures appropriately

  
3.

  
RESOURCE CLEANUP:

  
 •

  
Destroy per-client mutexes

  
 •

  
Clear thread handles

  
 •

  
Reset client thread state








GRACEFUL
  TERMINATION: 


THREAD COORDINATION:


    
  
  • Threads monitor running flags in their main loops
    • 
  
  • Flags are checked frequently (every iteration)
    • 
  
  • Threads exit cleanly within one processing cycle
    • 
  
  • No forced thread termination (unsafe)
    • 



DETERMINISTIC CLEANUP:


    
  
  • asciichat_thread_join() waits for complete thread exit
    • 
  
  • All thread resources are properly released
    • 
  
  • No zombie threads or resource leaks
    • 







ERROR
  HANDLING: 


THREAD JOIN FAILURES:


    
  
  • Logged as errors but don't prevent cleanup
    • 
  
  • Continue with resource cleanup regardless
    • 
  
  • Platform-specific error reporting
    • 



NULL CLIENT HANDLING:


    
  
  • Safe to call with NULL client pointer
    • 
  
  • Error logged and function returns safely
    • 
  
  • No undefined behavior or crashes
    • 







RESOURCE
  MANAGEMENT: 


MUTEX CLEANUP:


    
  
  • Destroys all per-client mutexes
    • 
  
  • Prevents resource leaks on client disconnect
    • 
  
  • Platform-independent destruction
    • 



THREAD HANDLE MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Clears thread handles after joining
    • 
  
  • Prevents accidental reuse of stale handles
    • 
  
  • Memory zeroing for safety
    • 







INTEGRATION
  REQUIREMENTS: 


    
  
  • Called by remove_client() in client.c
    • 
  
  • Must be called before freeing client structure
    • 
  
  • Coordinates with other cleanup functions
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for thread cleanup


Note


Function is safe to call multiple times (idempotent)



All resources are cleaned up regardless of errors




Thread joining may take up to one processing cycle




Warning


Must be called before client structure deallocation


See also


create_client_render_threads() For thread creation



asciichat_thread_join() For platform-specific joining




Definition at line 1352 of file server/render.c.


1352                                                        {
1353   if (!client) {
1354     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Client is NULL");
1355     return;
1356   }
1357 
1358   log_debug("Stopping render threads for client %s", client->client_id);
1359 
1360   // Signal threads to stop (atomic operations, no mutex needed)
1361   atomic_store_bool(&client->video_render_thread_running, false);
1362   atomic_store_bool(&client->audio_render_thread_running, false);
1363 
1364   // Wait for threads to finish (deterministic cleanup)
1365   // During shutdown, don't wait forever for threads to join
1366   bool is_shutting_down = atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
1367 
1368   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->video_render_thread)) {
1369     log_debug("Joining video render thread for client %u", client->client_id);
1370     int result;
1371     if (is_shutting_down) {
1372       // During shutdown, don't timeout - wait for thread to exit
1373       // Timeouts mask the real problem: threads that are still running
1374       log_debug("Shutdown mode: joining video render thread for client %u (no timeout)", client->client_id);
1375       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->video_render_thread, NULL);
1376       if (result != 0) {
1377         log_warn("Video render thread for client %u failed to join during shutdown: %s", client->client_id,
1378                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1379       }
1380     } else {
1381       log_debug("Calling asciichat_thread_join for video thread of client %u", client->client_id);
1382       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->video_render_thread, NULL);
1383       log_debug("asciichat_thread_join returned %d for video thread of client %u", result, client->client_id);
1384     }
1385 
1386     if (result == 0) {
1387 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1388       log_debug("Video render thread joined for client %u", client->client_id);
1389 #endif
1390     } else if (result != -2) { // Don't log timeout errors again
1391       if (is_shutting_down) {
1392         log_warn("Failed to join video render thread for client %u during shutdown (continuing): %s", client->client_id,
1393                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1394       } else {
1395         log_error("Failed to join video render thread for client %u: %s", client->client_id, SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1396       }
1397     }
1398     // Clear thread handle safely using platform abstraction
1399     asciichat_thread_init(&client->video_render_thread);
1400   }
1401 
1402   if (asciichat_thread_is_initialized(&client->audio_render_thread)) {
1403     int result;
1404     if (is_shutting_down) {
1405       // During shutdown, don't timeout - wait for thread to exit
1406       // Timeouts mask the real problem: threads that are still running
1407       log_debug("Shutdown mode: joining audio render thread for client %u (no timeout)", client->client_id);
1408       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->audio_render_thread, NULL);
1409       if (result != 0) {
1410         log_warn("Audio render thread for client %u failed to join during shutdown: %s", client->client_id,
1411                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1412       }
1413     } else {
1414       result = asciichat_thread_join(&client->audio_render_thread, NULL);
1415     }
1416 
1417     if (result == 0) {
1418 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1419       log_debug("Audio render thread joined for client %u", client->client_id);
1420 #endif
1421     } else if (result != -2) { // Don't log timeout errors again
1422       if (is_shutting_down) {
1423         log_warn("Failed to join audio render thread for client %u during shutdown (continuing): %s", client->client_id,
1424                  SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1425       } else {
1426         log_error("Failed to join audio render thread for client %u: %s", client->client_id, SAFE_STRERROR(result));
1427       }
1428     }
1429     // Clear thread handle safely using platform abstraction
1430     asciichat_thread_init(&client->audio_render_thread);
1431   }
1432 
1433   // DO NOT destroy the mutex here - client.c will handle it
1434   // mutex_destroy(&client->client_state_mutex);
1435 
1436 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1437   log_debug("Successfully destroyed render threads for client %u", client->client_id);
1438 #endif
1439 }






References asciichat_thread_init(),
    asciichat_thread_is_initialized(), asciichat_thread_join(),
    atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    client_info::audio_render_thread,
    client_info::audio_render_thread_running,
    client_info::client_id, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    g_should_exit, log_debug, log_error, log_warn,
    SAFE_STRERROR, SET_ERRNO,
    client_info::video_render_thread, and
    client_info::video_render_thread_running.

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
